description: You are a Workforce Management (WFM) Analyst for a telecommunications provider. Management needs data to justify hiring new agents to handle concurrent volume spikes and Service Level Agreement (SLA) breaches. Using the CALL_LOGS table in the WFM_ANALYST schema (User: wfm_analyst, Pass: Wfm2024, Service: XEPDB1, Host: localhost, Port: 1521), create a view named INTERVAL_SUMMARY_VW that groups calls into 15-minute intervals by start_time and queue_name. This view must include interval_start (formatted 'YYYY-MM-DD HH24:MI:SS'), queue_name, total_calls, abandoned_calls (where call_status is 'ABANDONED'), sla_breach_count (calls where wait time exceeded 60 seconds, calculated as answered_time - start_time, or end_time - start_time for abandoned calls), and service_level_pct. Additionally, create a view named PEAK_CONCURRENCY_VW that calculates the maximum simultaneous active calls for each day and queue (active from start_time to end_time), outputting call_date (truncated to day), queue_name, and max_concurrent_calls. Identify frustrated callers by creating a view named REPEAT_ABANDON_CALLERS_VW for instances where a caller abandoned a call and started a new call within 120 minutes of the first call's start_time; include caller_phone, abandoned_call_id, abandoned_time, callback_call_id, callback_time, and minutes_between_calls. Export the contents of INTERVAL_SUMMARY_VW to /home/ga/Documents/exports/wfm_interval_summary.csv.
